title: A dataset recording joint EEG-fMRI during affective music listening
description: A joint EEG-fMRI dataset acquired in 2016 from 21 healthy adult participants during affective music listening. Participants listened to music stimuli designed to elicit varying emotional responses and provided self-reported affective state ratings. This multimodal neuroimaging dataset enables investigation of the neural correlates of emotional responses to music across both electrophysiological and hemodynamic measures. Published in 2019.
methods_description: Data were collected in 2016 using simultaneous EEG-fMRI recording while 21 healthy adult participants listened to music stimuli selected to produce different affective responses. Participants self-reported their felt affective states during the listening task.
